is an American [string quartet](/wiki/String_quartet "String quartet") dedicated to the performance of [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music"). It was founded in 2005 and is based in New York City. The four founding members are violinists Christopher Otto and Ari Streisfeld, violist John Pickford Richards, and cellist Kevin McFarland.[[1]](#cite_note-JACKMembers-1) In 2016, violinist Austin Wulliman and cellist Jay Campbell joined the quartet, replacing Streisfeld and McFarland.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The quartet met while attending the [Eastman School of Music](/wiki/Eastman_School_of_Music "Eastman School of Music"), and they have studied closely with the [Kronos Quartet](/wiki/Kronos_Quartet "Kronos Quartet"), [Arditti Quartet](/wiki/Arditti_Quartet "Arditti Quartet"), and Muir String Quartet.[[3]](#cite_note-Eastman-3)

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Contemporary classical string quartet

**JACK Quartet** is an American [string quartet](/wiki/String_quartet "String quartet") dedicated to the performance of [contemporary classical music](/wiki/Contemporary_classical_music "Contemporary classical music"). It was founded in 2005 and is based in New York City. The four founding members are violinists Christopher Otto and Ari Streisfeld, violist John Pickford Richards, and cellist Kevin McFarland.[[1]](#cite_note-JACKMembers-1) In 2016, violinist Austin Wulliman and cellist Jay Campbell joined the quartet, replacing Streisfeld and McFarland.[[2]](#cite_note-2) The quartet met while attending the [Eastman School of Music](/wiki/Eastman_School_of_Music "Eastman School of Music"), and they have studied closely with the [Kronos Quartet](/wiki/Kronos_Quartet "Kronos Quartet"), [Arditti Quartet](/wiki/Arditti_Quartet "Arditti Quartet"), and Muir String Quartet.[[3]](#cite_note-Eastman-3)

JACK has received strong critical reception for their performances of quartets by [Iannis Xenakis](/wiki/Iannis_Xenakis "Iannis Xenakis"),[[4]](#cite_note-NewYorker-4) [Georg Friedrich Haas](/wiki/Georg_Friedrich_Haas "Georg Friedrich Haas"),[[5]](#cite_note-LATimesHaas-5) [Helmut Lachenmann](/wiki/Helmut_Lachenmann "Helmut Lachenmann")[[6]](#cite_note-NYTLachenmann-6) and [Steve Reich](/wiki/Steve_Reich "Steve Reich"),[[7]](#cite_note-NYTReich-7) and has performed at venues around the world, including Carnegie Hall, New York's Lincoln Center, London's Wigmore Hall,[[8]](#cite_note-Wigmore-8) and Amsterdam's Muziekgebouw. The group has also been heard at many contemporary music festivals, including the [Donaueschingen Festival](/wiki/Donaueschingen_Festival "Donaueschingen Festival"), the [Lucerne Festival](/wiki/Lucerne_Festival "Lucerne Festival"), the Bali Arts Festival, the [Reykjavik Arts Festival](/wiki/Reykjav%C3%ADk_Arts_Festival "Reykjavík Arts Festival"), and the [Venice Biennale](/wiki/Venice_Biennale "Venice Biennale").[[9]](#cite_note-Lucerne-9)

JACK Quartet's radio credits include appearances on WNYC's New Sounds and Soundcheck,[[10]](#cite_note-Soundcheck-10) "The Music Show" from Australian National Broadcasting,[[11]](#cite_note-ABC-11) and a full concert broadcast by NPR.[[12]](#cite_note-NPR-12)

The group has initiated many educational residencies with composition students from a variety of institutions including New York University, Columbia University, The University of Iowa and Berklee College of Music.[[13]](#cite_note-Iowa-13)

Their album *Lines Made By Walking* was nominated for a [Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance](/wiki/Grammy_Award_for_Best_Chamber_Music/Small_Ensemble_Performance "Grammy Award for Best Chamber Music/Small Ensemble Performance") at the [2022 Grammy Awards](/wiki/64th_Annual_Grammy_Awards "64th Annual Grammy Awards") ceremony.[[14]](#cite_note-14)

## Quartet members

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=JACK_Quartet&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Quartet members")]

* Christopher Otto, [violin](/wiki/Violin "Violin")
* Austin Wulliman, [violin](/wiki/Violin "Violin")
* John Pickford Richards, [viola](/wiki/Viola "Viola")
* Jay Campbell, [cello](/wiki/Cello "Cello")

## Recordings

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=JACK_Quartet&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Recordings")]

***rag'sma***

* Released: 2021
* Format: LP + digital
* Label: [greyfade](/wiki/Greyfade "Greyfade")
* Writer: Christopher Otto
* Tracks: rag'sma q1q2q3, rag'sma q1q2, rag'sma q1/q2

***A Cockroach's Tarantella***

* Released: 2020
* Format: Streaming
* Label: [Modern Sky](/wiki/Modern_Sky "Modern Sky")
* Writer: [Du Yun](/wiki/Du_Yun "Du Yun")
* Tracks: Epilogue, A Cockroach's Tarantella - English, Tattooed In Snow, A Cockroach's Tarantella - Chinese, Prologue. A Cockroach's Tarantella (with [Du Yun](/wiki/Du_Yun "Du Yun"), narrator)

***Hidden***

* Released: 2018
* Format: CD
* Label: WERGO
* Writer: [Chaya Czernowin](/wiki/Chaya_Czernowin "Chaya Czernowin")
* Tracks: Hidden

***Laura Schwendinger: Quartets***

* Released: 2018
* Format: CD
* Label: [Albany Records](/wiki/Albany_Records "Albany Records")
* Writer: [Laura Schwendinger](/wiki/Laura_Schwendinger "Laura Schwendinger")
* Tracks: Creature Quartet: Hymn for Lost Creatures (JACK Quartet), Sudden Light (Jamie Van Eyck, mezzo soprano; Christopher Otto, violin; Ari Streisfeld, violin; Kevin McFarland, cello), String Quartet in three movements (JACK Quartet), Song for Andrew (Christopher Otto, violin; John Richards, viola; Kevin McFarland, cello; Christopher Taylor, piano)

***First Performance IV***

* Released: 2014
* Format: CD
* Tracks: Robericus/Christopher Otto: Angelorum Psalat, Elliot Carter: String Quartet No. 3, [Ruth Crawford Seeger](/wiki/Ruth_Crawford_Seeger "Ruth Crawford Seeger"): String Quartet, George Freiderich Haas: String Quartet No. 8

***Cineshape & Duos***

* Released: 2013
* Format: CD
* Label: [Albany Records](/wiki/Albany_Records "Albany Records")
* Writer: [Amy Williams](/wiki/Amy_Williams_(composer) "Amy Williams (composer)")
* Tracks: First Lines (Lindsey Goodman, flute; Amy Williams, piano), Stop/Yield (Kevin McFarland, cello; Amy Williams, piano), Wrest (Ari Streisfeld, violin; Amy Williams, piano), Duo (Noah Getz, saxophone; Andrew Welch, piano), Cineshape 1 (Lindsey Goodman, flute; Scott Christian, percussion), Cineshape 3 (Lindsey Goodman, flute; Kevin McFarland, cello; Scott Christian, percussion), Cineshape 4 (Amy Williams, piano), Cineshape 5 (Lindsey Goodman, flute; JACK Quartet)

***Crossings***

* Released: 2013
* Format: CD
* Label: [Albany Records](/wiki/Albany_Records "Albany Records")
* Writer: [Amy Williams](/wiki/Amy_Williams_(composer) "Amy Williams (composer)")
* Tracks: Richer Textures, Brigid's Flame, Falling, Astoria, Crossings, Abstracted Art, Cineshape 2

***Threadsuns***

* Released: 2011
* Format: CD
* Label: [Tzadik Records](/wiki/Tzadik_Records "Tzadik Records")
* Writer: Ha-Yang Kim
